papers.title_en: Domain Arithmetic: One-Shot VLA Adaptation under Environmental Shifts
papers.authors: Taewook Kang, Taeheon Kim, Donghyun Shin, Jonghyun Choi
papers.evidence: 提出面向视觉-语言-动作模型的单次域适应方法
papers.tldr: 视觉-语言-动作模型在环境变化（如相机位姿、机器人类型改变）下性能下降。现有适应方法需多演示数据，成本高。本文提出DART方法，仅需单次演示，通过权重向量算术与子空间对齐实现领域适应。在模拟和真实实验中，DART在单样本场景下优于现有方法，大幅降低数据收集负担。
papers.abstract_en: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models often fail to perform the same learned tasks under environmental shifts, such as changes in camera pose and shifts to a different but similar robot (e.g., from Panda to UR5e). Adapting these models to the shifted environment (i.e., target domain) often requires training on multiple demonstrations for each task, which are costly to collect. To reduce the burden of data curation and training, we propose an analogy-based method that adapts VLA models under environmental shifts through weight vector arithmetic with domain-specific information addition, named Domain ARiThmetic (DART). Unlike prior approaches, DART requires collecting only a single demonstration, enabling efficient adaptation. To accurately isolate domain-specific information for addition, DART performs subspace alignment between singular components in weight vectors to filter out noisy components. In both simulated and real-world experiments, DART outperforms existing VLA adaptation methods in one-shot scenarios across diverse visual and embodiment shifts. Code is available at https://github.com/snumprlab/dart.
